No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration found under this name; committee-level FEC detail was not retrievable through public web sources, so no federal PAC affiliation is asserted.

affiliations.role: Senior Director of Major Gifts (employee; fundraising leadership)

name: Washington, Milton

name: Garcia, Lorraine
resolved_employer: Travis County District Attorney's Office — Assistant District Attorney ('TCDA' confirmed = Travis County District Attorney)
evidence: State Bar of Texas record for Lorraine M. Garcia (bar card 24125608, licensed 10/11/2021, Texas A&M School of Law JD 2021, no public disciplinary history) lists her employer as the Travis County District Attorney's Office at 416 W 11th St, Austin, TX 78701 — confirming the filed employer string 'TCDA' and occupation 'Attorney'. Recipient Amber Goodwin is herself a Travis County assistant district attorney, corroborating a colleague-donation pattern. Not found in Texas Ethics Commission registered-lobbyist lists; no federal PAC affiliation surfaced in FEC-oriented searches.

name: Confer, Alexis
resolved_employer: Confer Strategies LLC — independent strategic consultant to movement-building organizations (Brooklyn, NY)
evidence: Alexis Confer is a Brooklyn-based progressive strategist who runs her own consulting firm serving movement-building organizations, matching the filed occupation 'Strategic Consultant' / employer 'Confer Strategies LLC' and Brooklyn 11205 address. She was the inaugural Executive Director of March For Our Lives (after four years as its Director of Development) and earlier the first National Field Director for Everytown for Gun Safety, plus roles at the NYC Mayor's Office (2014-2020) and Obama for America 2012. Her gun-violence-prevention background corroborates the donation to Amber Goodwin, founder of the Community Justice Action Fund. Not in the Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registry (out-of-state donor); no federal PAC contributions surfaced.

affiliations.role: Executive Director (inaugural); previously Director of Development
affiliations.snippet: Statement attributed to 'March for Our Lives Executive Director' Alexis Confer: 'The powerful not only can act, they must act, and our plan shows them how... it's time for the powerful to deliver bold change.'

Published participant bio: 'Prior to joining City Hall, Alexis served as the first National Field Director for Everytown for Gun Safety, as well as the Deputy Field Director in Pennsylvania for Obama for America 2012.'

name: Cavazos, Perla

evidence: Perla Cavazos is Deputy Administrator and Chief Governance & Government Affairs Officer at Central Health, matching the filing's occupation ('Deputy Administrator') and employer ('Central Health') exactly; she is a longtime Austin resident (78702 is East Austin, inside District 1) with a 25+ year public-policy career including the Texas Senate, Texas Legal Services Center, and Sendero Health Plans, and she was a 2009 Austin City Council Place 1 candidate.

name: Jackson, Gregory
resolved_employer: Community Justice / Community Justice Action Fund — Executive Advisor (also President, The Rocket Foundation; Partner, Safer Futures Consulting)
evidence: Confirmed as Gregory Jackson Jr., the gun-violence-prevention advocate: the filing's employer ('Community Justice') and occupation ('Senior Adviser') match his publicly announced return to Community Justice as Executive Advisor after serving as Deputy Director of the White House Office of Gun Violence Prevention, and the 20020 ZIP is Southeast Washington, DC, where he lives and where he survived a 2013 shooting. He is also a 2026 DC Council At-Large candidate. Recipient Amber Goodwin is herself a gun-violence-prevention organizer, corroborating the professional-network donation pattern.

affiliations.snippet: Community Justice announced Jackson's return as Executive Advisor; he previously led CJAF as Executive Director, the first Black male gun-violence survivor to lead a national gun-violence-prevention policy organization.
Congressional witness bio: 'Gregory Jackson is the President of the Rocket Foundation, Adviser to Community Justice Action Fund, and Partner of Safer Futures Consulting Firm.'

affiliations.role: Former Deputy Director and Special Assistant to President Biden
affiliations.snippet: Bio states he served as Deputy Director of the White House Office of Gun Violence Prevention, overseeing implementation of the Bipartisan Safer Communities Act and community violence intervention funding.

affiliations.snippet: WTOP profiles Greg Jackson, former Biden White House gun-violence-prevention adviser, as a candidate for the DC Council At-Large seat.

name: Hyams, Chris
resolved_employer: Indeed — Chief Executive Officer 2019-2025 (previously President and VP of Product); now former CEO
evidence: Chris Hyams was CEO of Austin-headquartered job platform Indeed from 2019 to 2025, matching the filing's occupation ('CEO'), employer ('Indeed'), Austin 78731 location, and the 2022 gift date falling inside his CEO tenure. He joined Indeed in 2010 as VP of Product, became President in 2015, and earlier founded the film-analytics company B-Side and was VP of Engineering at Trilogy Software. No TEC lobbyist registration and no ideological-PAC federal giving surfaced.

affiliations.snippet: Bio notes he is a board member at DivInc, a technology accelerator whose mission is to generate social and economic equity through entrepreneurship.
Bio lists board membership at Huston-Tillotson University, a Historically Black University and Austin's oldest institution of higher learning.

Saldana, president/principal of Saldana Public Relations in Austin; former Austin ISD Trustee for District 6 (2015-2017) and Board Vice-President (2016-17), and former chief of staff to Austin Mayor Gus Garcia. evidence: Nadia Garnett is a partner at Deliver Strategies, a Democratic direct-mail/voter-contact firm, and president of Oliver and Lane; she was African American vote director on Hillary Clinton's 2016 campaign and previously worked for the DNC, DCCC and EMILY's List. Employer string ('Deliver Strategies'), occupation ('Political Consultant') and Washington, DC location all match. NBC News reported the Clinton campaign's expansion of its African-American staff, naming a Black vote director; Garnett's bio pages identify her as the campaign's African American vote director.

evidence: Fernandez Anderson is the founder and principal consultant of Majestic Settlement Consultants, LLC, a structured-settlement and settlement-planning firm serving plaintiff attorneys in the Southeast; he holds a Certified Structured Settlement Consultant designation (Notre Dame Mendoza) and spent his earlier career at New York Life and Dean Witter Reynolds. Firm name, 'Founder and CEO' occupation and metro-Atlanta (Alpharetta 30009) location all match. name: Anderson, Greg
resolved_employer: Austin Habitat for Humanity — Director of Community Affairs
evidence: Greg Anderson is Austin Habitat for Humanity's director of community affairs (joined Jan 2015 after five years as policy director for Austin Mayor Pro Tem Sheryl Cole) and a longtime City of Austin Planning Commission member; the donor's self-reported occupation 'Affordable Homeownership' and employer 'Austin Habitat for Humanity' match exactly, and 78701 is downtown Austin. He is a well-known pro-housing/pro-transit voice in Austin land-use debates. FEC individual-contribution and TEC lobbyist searches returned no records under this name matching Austin/Habitat; his City of Austin board application states he is not a registered lobbyist with the City.

affiliations.snippet: City Council approved Anderson's appointment to the Planning Commission again; he was among eight commissioners AG Ken Paxton's office sought to remove in 2018 under the rule barring members connected with real estate and land development.

affiliations.role: Director of Community Affairs; leads regional advocacy for affordable housing and zoning-code reform
affiliations.snippet: Anderson is responsible for expanding Austin Habitat's product line to include multifamily properties and leading regional advocacy efforts for affordable housing; he convened housing leaders to organize a coalition ahead of a major affordable-housing bond campaign.

name: Plummer, Junie Marie
resolved_employer: City of Austin — parkland/conservation land acquisition, 30+ years (retired); TREC-licensed real estate provider; former title-company employee
evidence: Distinctive full-name match corroborated three ways: a Texas Real Estate Commission (TREC) license record for 'Junie Marie Plummer' matches the reported 'real estate' occupation, and a Texas Legacy oral-history transcript for the same name describes an Austin resident who worked years in the title business, then spent over 30 years acquiring parkland for the City of Austin (Balcones Canyonlands HCP, water-quality and open-space deals, ~60,000 acres) before retiring — matching the reported 'retired' employer. Her Nature Conservancy and U.S. Fish & Wildlife dealings were as the City's public-sector land negotiator, not as an org board member.

name: Almanza, Susana
resolved_employer: PODER (People Organized in Defense of Earth and Her Resources) — co-founder and Director
evidence: Reported occupation 'Director' and employer 'PODER' match exactly a well-documented East Austin public-record figure: Susana Almanza co-founded PODER in 1991 and serves as its director. She is a longtime environmental-justice organizer, a two-time Austin City Council District 3 candidate (2014, 2018), and a 2021 appointee to the National Environmental Justice Advisory Council. Her documented advocacy is environmental justice, anti-gentrification and land use in East Austin; no federal PAC, lobbyist-registry, or Israel/Palestine, firearms, oil-and-gas, or defense-industry affiliations were found on either side of those spectrums.

affiliations.org: PODER (People Organized in Defense of Earth and Her Resources)
affiliations.snippet: Susana Almanza is a founding member and Director of PODER, a woman-led people-of-color organization engaging the local community in environmental and economic issues through education, advocacy and action.

affiliations.snippet: Co-chair for the Southwest Network for Environmental & Economic Justice Coordinating Council, a network of over 80 grassroots organizations in the Southwest U.S.; she also helped found the Texas Network for Environmental and Economic Justice.
In 2021 Almanza was appointed to the National Environmental Justice Advisory Council.

affiliations.org: City of Austin boards and commissions

affiliations.snippet: Other previous experience includes working on the City of Austin's Planning Commission, the Parks and Recreation Board, the Environmental Board, and the Community Development Commission.

name: Madhav, Mira
evidence: Self-reported occupation 'VOLUNTEER' with employer 'N/A' and no federal donation history. All three mandatory searches were run and returned nothing: no FEC individual-contribution or PAC records, no Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration, and no bio, professional profile, firm page, or organizational leadership listing for a Mira Madhav in Austin. The only hits were commercial people-search aggregator entries for a 'Mira T Madhav' in the 78746 zip, which are not a reliable public-record source and do not establish employment, role, or affiliation — so nothing is classified beyond the self-reported non-employment status.
